title: A Town of 7,000 Is Voting for Mayor — With a Blank Ballot
script: The village of Bayville on Long Island has 7,000 residents and a mayoral election on June 16. There is just one problem: nobody ran. The sitting mayor stepped down. No one else filed by the deadline. Officials say this has never happened before in Bayville's history. On election day, residents will show up to a completely blank ballot. The next mayor will be decided entirely by write-in votes. The job pays $5,000 a year.
